chain_edges.notes: Big-3 default-on MCP allow/block enforcement creates the parallel-schema pressure that forces convergence on a published interop RFC.
Hyperscaler-AI-lab compute lock-in makes cross-cloud agent-identity interop the only way enterprises can avoid single-vendor capture.
Pentagon-Anthropic exclusion + lab carve-outs supply the regulatory-pull asymmetry that forces BIS/NIST/EO export-control category definition.
CSAI MITRE-CNA + ATT&CK v19 named techniques are the disclosure-side regulatory infrastructure cited as scaffolding for the export-control category.
Broader AI-capex 10-Q footnote disclosure pressure feeds the same buyside-attention threshold logic into physical-AI capex segmentation.
AI-revenue disclosure rewrite establishes the Sept-30 10-Q as the venue where physical-AI capex breakout naturally lands.
Cerebras IPO mid-May pricing + S-1 disclosure is the operative anchor signal feeding the buyside-pressure stack the focal prediction cites.
Apr 28 WSJ OpenAI-miss disclosure-rewrite arc feeds directly into the SEC concept-release pressure curve.

relations.notes: Both extend the May 1 Microsoft Agent 365 GA into a cross-cloud standardization wave; this one targets the schema/RFC layer, the other targets policy enforcement.
Independent multi-cloud standardization facets: agent-identity + MCP inventory RFC vs per-model SLA disclosure standardization.
Both target agent-as-first-class-identity registration; this one via cross-cloud RFC, the other via US state statutory mandate.
Both flow from May 1 Pentagon-Anthropic dynamics; export-control category vs lab-side ToS carve-outs are independent facets.
Frontier export-control regulation vs hyperscaler MCP-policy enforcement — different governance layers, both derivative of agentic-AI build-out.
Export-control category vs CSAI-CNA CVE sub-corpus + marketplace gate are parallel regulatory tracks sharing the CSAI/ATT&CK disclosure substrate.
Frontier-model export licensing vs agent-skills threat sub-matrix; both are 2026 governance-gap closures triggered by ATT&CK v19 technique naming.
Same-day siblings: export-control category vs cross-cloud agent-identity/MCP registry RFC are independent governance facets.
Federal export-control category vs US-state Statutory Identity for Autonomous Agents Act — parallel federal/state agent-governance tracks.
Both forecast 10-Q footnote AI disclosure under buyside pressure; focal is physical-AI capex, peer is broader AI-business KPIs.
Parallel disclosure-pressure facet: AI-revenue / per-token margin disclosure vs physical-AI capex segmentation, both Mag-7-driven.
Same disclosure-rewrite thesis on a different revenue/cost surface; not competing outcomes.
Per-token-margin-gated capex disclosure and physical-AI capex segmentation are independent facets of the Mag-7 disclosure shift.
Both anchor on Mag-7 capex tape forcing new disclosure modes; one targets per-token margin gates, the other physical-AI capex.
Same-day sibling; both predict 10-Q footnote disclosure split-out, focal on AI-services KPI vs peer on physical-AI capex segments.
Both predict the same 2026 SEC + analyst push for AI-revenue disclosure with hyperscaler 10-Q AI-segment footnote; near paraphrase.
Both center on hyperscaler AI-economics disclosure under $700B-capex pressure; focal targets SEC rule, peer targets per-token-margin capex gate.
Same Mag-7 capex / ROI-reckoning framing; peer focuses on hyperscaler tiering vs focal SEC concept release.
Both ride the post-Apr 27 contract-rewrite + disclosure-shape arc; peer covers AGI-clause template, focal covers SEC AI-revenue rule.
